title: Seville 3-Day Cultural Tour
metaTitle: Seville 3-Day Cultural Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 3-day trip to Seville, Spain: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Real Alcázar of Seville and Seville Cathedral & Giralda Tower. Prices from $953 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 3 days discovering Seville, Spain — One of Europe's hottest cities in summer, with highs around 36-37°C in July-August, and mild winters with highs around 16°C in January. March to May and October to November for pleasant temperatures before and after the intense summer heat. This itinerary blends Real Alcázar of Seville, Seville Cathedral & Giralda Tower, Plaza de España with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
2 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Real Alcázar of Seville, Seville Cathedral & Giralda Tower, Plaza de España
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Seville
itinerary.description: Land at Seville Airport (SVQ) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Real Alcázar of Seville — Stunning Mudéjar royal palace and gardens. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Seville: Seville Cathedral & Giralda Tower & Casa de Pilatos
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Seville Cathedral & Giralda Tower — World's largest Gothic cathedral with a climbable tower. In the afternoon, continue to Casa de Pilatos — Andalusian palace blending Mudéjar and Renaissance styles. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Metropol Parasol (Las Setas) or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to SVQ for your departure flight.

travelTips: Avoid outdoor sightseeing during peak heat in July-August afternoons — go early morning or evening instead.
Book Real Alcázar tickets online for a timed entry slot.
Dinner reservations are rarely needed before 9pm as locals eat late.
Feria de Abril and Semana Santa require accommodation booked many months ahead.
A tapas crawl — bar to bar for small plates — is the most authentic and affordable way to eat.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Lightweight breathable clothing for intense summer heat
A hat and high-SPF sunscreen for shade-free plazas
A light scarf or shawl for cooler evenings and church visits
Comfortable shoes for cobblestones in Barrio Santa Cruz
thingsToAvoid: Visiting in July-August without adjusting your schedule around extreme heat.
Assuming shops are open during traditional afternoon siesta hours.
Overpaying for flamenco shows aimed purely at tour groups — seek out a proper tablao.
Wearing shorts or tank tops into the Cathedral or Alcázar.
Walking quieter alleys of Barrio Santa Cruz late at night without awareness of surroundings.
instagramSpots: Giralda Tower from Plaza del Triunfo
familyActivities: Plaza de España rowing boat rentals
Parque de María Luisa horse-carriage rides
adventureActivities: Horseback riding in the Andalusian countryside
Hot air balloon ride over Seville's countryside
luxuryExperiences: Private flamenco performance at a historic tablao
Suite stay at Hotel Alfonso XIII
Private guided tour of the Real Alcázar before opening
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset horse-drawn carriage ride through Parque de María Luisa
Romantic dinner in Barrio Santa Cruz
Evening stroll along the Guadalquivir riverside
budgetExperiences: Free wander through Barrio Santa Cruz and Plaza de España
Free flamenco street performances in Triana

festivalCalendar.name: Feria de Abril
festivalCalendar.timing: Two weeks after Easter, flamenco dresses and horse-drawn carriages

festivalCalendar.name: Corpus Christi
festivalCalendar.timing: May/June, religious procession with flower carpets

localEtiquette: Traditional siesta hours (roughly 2-5pm) mean many small shops close in early afternoon.
Dinner is eaten late, often after 9-10pm.
Flamenco shows are a serious art form — stay respectfully quiet during performances.
Dress modestly in the Cathedral and Real Alcázar.
Greet acquaintances with two cheek kisses.
localTransportGuide: Seville is famously flat and one of Europe's most bike-friendly cities, with the SEVICI public bike-share system and dedicated cycle lanes throughout. The compact historic centre is best explored on foot, while a single tram line and bus network cover longer distances including to Santa Justa train station.
bestTimeToVisit: March to May and October to November for pleasant temperatures before and after the intense summer heat.
weather: One of Europe's hottest cities in summer, with highs around 36-37°C in July-August, and mild winters with highs around 16°C in January.
visaInformation: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, need a Schengen visa arranged in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~11h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Seville?
faqs.answer: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, need a Schengen visa arranged in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Seville?
faqs.answer: March to May and October to November for pleasant temperatures before and after the intense summer heat. One of Europe's hottest cities in summer, with highs around 36-37°C in July-August, and mild winters with highs around 16°C in January.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Seville safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Seville is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Seville?
faqs.answer: The local currency is EUR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Daniel Sharma
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Flamenco Dance Museum and Palacio de las Dueñas. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Noah van Dijk
reviews.title: Loved every moment
reviews.body: Our trip to Seville was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Puente de Isabel II (Triana Bridge) was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Omar Dubois
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Flamenco show at a tablao and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Priya Müller
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Booked this as a group trip and it was well paced. Guadalquivir riverside promenade in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Ava Johnson
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Seville Cathedral & Giralda Tower alone was worth the trip, but combined with Plaza de España this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Tom Botha
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Triana district and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.
